reasoning: The base rate for any team winning the World Series is roughly 1/30 (3.3%), but the Pittsburgh Pirates are historically uncompetitive. The Polymarket price in late May 2025 was 1%. Sportsbook odds evaluated prior to the season and into the early season showed extremely long odds (+12000 to +19000). To confirm their current status, search results for late May / early June 2025 show that the Pirates are having a terrible season. They were 21-37 around May 31, ranked 28th out of 30 teams in power rankings, and on pace for over 100 losses (Fox Sports evaluated them at 22-38 on June 2, on pace for 103 losses). There are reports of them being the worst offense in MLB, closer to the historically bad Colorado Rockies than to a wildcard spot. Their probability of winning the 2025 World Series is negligible, likely below 0.1%, but minimum forecast rules often prefer bounding near extreme asymptotes. The Polymarket limit price of 0.01 makes a prediction of exactly the floor limit (0.02) very well-calibrated for an almost certain 'No'.
